sutta: liberated both ways liberated both ways he is called sir and as to what has the 1 liberated boat was being declared by the exalted 1 sir the monk who aloo from sen's desire enters and abides in the first jhana and to the extent of that sphere abides with body attune to it and by wisdom understands it thus faster in 1 particular has the 1 liberated by uh liberated both ways being declared by the exalted 1 similarly etc enters and who with the stealing of applied and sustained thought enters and abides in the second jhana the third jannah fourth jana and the other jana's and abides with body attune to it and by wisdom understands it and then in that particular has the 1 liberated broadway is being declared by the exalted 1 so here this utah is talking about the ubato bhagavato 1 who is liberated by both ways this 1 who is liberated by both ways means that he is an arahant liberated by wisdom and liberated by mind now if you compare with other sutas you find that this person is an arahant liberated by wisdom and by mind now in certain sutas he is also said to be 1 liberated by mind liberated by mind these are all the same liberated both ways liberated by wisdom and by mind and liberated by mind these are all the same and what it means is that this person at the moment of liberation he is liberated by wisdom and by mind at the same time what it means is this person attained he attains the jhanas and after attaining the jhanas he used that still mine too like the buddha contemplate the past lives the buddha attained the 3 knowledges first knowledge was the recollection of past lives the second 1 was the using the deva eye seeing beings dying and re-being reborn according to karma and that's the second knowledge the third 1 is the contemplation of the 4 noble truths and from there he attains hood so this 1 liberated both ways is the 1 who attains the janus and using that still state after the janus to contemplate and attain liberation in other words during meditation a person who attains a jaanas during meditation and becomes an arahan is 1 who is liberated both ways different from the 1 who is liberated by wisdom who just listens or contemplates without attaining the jaanas and attains liberation
